channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Police rescue five-year-old kidnap victim, arrest two suspects in Oyo
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Oyo State Police Command successfully rescued a five-year-old kidnap victim and arrested two suspects who demanded a N10 million ransom. Read more on the O
Read More: https://punchng.com/police-rescue-five-year-old-kidnap-victim-arrest-two-suspects-in-oyo/

item.title: Up to 45% of dementia cases can be prevented — WHO
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The WHO states that up to 45% of dementia cases can be prevented by tackling lifestyle and environmental risk factors. Learn how to prevent dementia.
Read More: https://punchng.com/up-to-45-of-dementia-cases-can-be-prevented-who/

item.title: VIDEO: Sanwo-Olu visits flooded Lagos communities
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Governor Sanwo-Olu assesses flood-hit communities in Lagos, interacting with residents and promising long-term flood control measures. Watch the video.
Read More: https://punchng.com/video-sanwo-olu-visits-flooded-lagos-communities/

item.title: Soldiers rescue 13 kidnap victims, foil terrorist operations in Zamfara
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Nigerian troops successfully rescued 13 kidnap victims and foiled planned terrorist operations in Zamfara State. Read more about the daring rescue.
Read More: https://punchng.com/soldiers-rescue-13-kidnap-victims-foil-terrorist-operations-in-zamfara/

item.title: Blessing CEO’s N36m Fraud Trial Stalled Over Illness
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The trial of social media influencer and relationship therapist Okoro Blessing Nkiruka, popularly known as Blessing CEO, was adjourned on Wednesday after she failed to appear before the Federal High Court in Ikoyi, Lagos, due to illness. Blessing CEO is being prosecuted by the Economic and Financial

item.title: Ododo Mobilises Security Operation To Rescue Abducted School Principal, NECO Official, Students
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Kogi State Government has mobilised a massive multi-agency security operation to secure the safe rescue of four persons abducted from Government Secondary School (GSS), Olowa, in Dekina Local Government Area during the ongoing National Examinations Council (NECO) examinations. The operation, dir

item.title: Nigeria Joins ICAO System To End Passport Authentication Delays
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The Federal Government has moved to eliminate authentication delays affecting Nigerian passport holders abroad after integrating the country’s Signing Certificate Authorities (CSCAs) into the International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO) public key directory. This means Nigerian passports c

item.title: My husband and I are retired. Our financial adviser, who is in his 30s, called us ‘you guyses.’ Is that unprofessional?
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: “I’m wondering whether I should ask to work with someone closer to our generation.”

item.title: Welfare: FG Pledges Nationwide Housing For Immigration Personnel
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Nigeria Immigration Service (NIS) has announced plans to provide its personnel across the country with decent accommodations, as part of measures to enhance service delivery. The Comptroller General of Immigration (CGIS), Kemi Nandap, made this disclosure on Wednesday during the inauguration of

item.title: Army urges Nigerians to stop demoralising troops with false social media narratives
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Anele said the increasing spread of misinformation and disinformation on social media had become a major challenge to national security. The post Army urges Nigerians to stop demoralising troops with false social media narratives appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Conor Benn will challenge Ryan Garcia for the WBC welterweight title in Las Vegas on September 12.

item.description: —To collaborate with UN to address porous borders in West Africa By Johnbosco Agbakwuru ABUJA — PRESIDENT Bola Tinubu has confirmed he will attend this year’s United Nations General Assembly, UNGA, in New York and deliver a high-profile speech on Nigeria’s reform successes. item.title: BUA to expand production after N504bn dividend
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
BUA Foods plans significant expansion in flour, pasta, and edible oils after a N504bn dividend, aiming to boost local food production and affordability.
Read More: https://punchng.com/bua-to-expand-production-after-n504bn-dividend/

item.title: 2027: Gov Kefas drops deputy, picks APC women leader as running mate
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Taraba State Governor, Agbu Kefas, has announced the All Progressives Congress Deputy National Women Leader, Hajiya Zainab Abubakar Ibrahim, as his running mate for the 2027 governorship race.With the development, incumbent Deputy Governor, Aminu Abdullahi Alkali, has been edged out of the race.

item.title: Police bust kidnapping gang in Osun, rescue victim
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: ‎"Upon receipt of the report, police operatives, in collaboration with local vigilantes, immediately mobilised to the scene and combed the surrounding forests and adjoining pathways in an effort to trace and rescue the victim."

item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The National Drug Law Enforcement Agency (NDLEA) has announced the conviction of a 28-year-old man who was found guilty of advertising cannabis for sale on social media. In a statement issued on Wednesday by the Kano Strategic Command’s Media and Advocacy Officer, Sadiq Muhammad Maigatari,

item.title: Family Fixes July 17 To Bury Physiotherapist Found Dead In Umahi’s House
item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria
item.description: The family of Mary Habila, popularly known as Mara, has announced July 17, 2026, as the burial date for the 26-year-old physiotherapist who was found dead at the Ebonyi State residence of the Minister of Works, David Umahi. Mary Habila reportedly died on June 27, 2026. Her death attracted public att

item.title: Police foil kidnap plot, nab suspected fraud kingpin in Anambra
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Operatives of the Anambra Police Command have foiled a plot by a gang of gunmen to kidnap some notable personalities in the state. Also, the Command arrested the leader of a three-man gang notorious for criminal activity operating between Anambra and Enugu states for defrauding unsuspecting members

item.title: State Police Funding Must Be Constitutionally Guaranteed, Says Bamidele
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The leader of the Senate, Opeyemi Bamidele, has said that the proposed state police structure must enjoy constitutionally guaranteed funding as a first-line charge. This, according to a statement credited to him on Wednesday, will prevent abuse by political actors, business interests, criminal eleme

item.title: NIS Unveils Nationwide Housing Drive, Commissions New Staff Estates
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Nigeria Immigration Service (NIS) has unveiled an ambitious nationwide housing programme for its personnel, declaring that the newly commissioned estates in the Federal Capital Territory are only the beginning of a broader plan to deliver affordable home ownership to officers across the country.

item.title: Headline Inflation Eases To 15.91% In June As Food Prices Remain Elevated
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The National Bureau of Statistics (NBS) in its latest Consumer Price Index (CPI) report said Nigeria’s headline inflation rate eased slightly to 15.91 per cent in June 2026, down from 15.93 per cent recorded in May. The NBS said the marginal decline reflects a modest slowdown in the pace of ri

item.description: This season's winner will walk away with a record ₦160 million grand prize, which includes a cash sum of ₦100 million and a brand-new SUV. The prize is the highest in the show's history, raising the stakes for what is expected to be another landmark season.

item.title: How teenage sensation built 15m-stream debut through authentic storytelling
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: In the rapidly evolving Nigerian music scene, 19-year-old artist Agboola Emmanuel Itunu, known as Abefe, has captured the hearts of read more How teenage sensation built 15m-stream debut through authentic storytelling

item.title: Delta: 20-year-old girl commits suicide after allegedly raped by content creator
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Delta State Police Command has arrested popular content creator Ifeanyi Ogbonna, popularly known as “Odogwu of Asaba,” over the alleged rape of a 20-year-old girl identified simply as Favour, who later died by suicide. The command’s spokesperson, SP Bright Edafe, disclosed this in a statement is

item.title: Kwara fire service saves shops from midnight fire
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Kwara State Fire firefighters in the early hours of Wednesday successfully curtailed a fire outbreak at a commercial building in the Tanke area of Ilorin, preventing what could have resulted in extensive property loss. According to the spokesman of the fire service, Hassan Adekunle, the service rece

item.title: Police bust Bauchi car theft syndicate, recover two vehicles
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Bauchi State Police Command says it has uncovered a suspected car theft syndicate, with the arrest of two suspects and the recovery of two vehicles believed to have been stolen. The command said the arrests followed an intelligence-led operation carried out by operatives of the Surveillance and

item.title: US Debt Clock Live
item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: Article URL: https://www.us-debt-clock.com/ Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48923984 Points: 5 # Comments: 0

item.title: Argentine VP calls English ‘pirates’ ahead of World Cup semi-final
item.dc:creator: Vanguard Sports
item.description: Argentina's Vice President Victoria Villarruel called the English "usurping pirates" ahead of Wednesday's World Cup semi-final between the two countries' teams. The post Argentine VP calls English ‘pirates’ ahead of World Cup semi-final appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Food diversion menace in Yobe: Govt seeks security support
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Machina insisted that it became imperative for the state to put a stop to theft of the life-saving therapeutic food meant for vulnerable children.

item.title: Spiro bets on football to drive electric mobility adoption in Kenya with Gor Mahia FC partnership
item.dc:creator: Technext
item.description: Leading electric mobility company in Africa, Spiro is partnering with Kenya’s most successful football club, Gor Mahia FC,…

item.title: Kogi School Abduction: Bandits have taken over Nigerian schools – Atiku
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The former Vice President and Presidential Candidate of the African Democratic Congress, Atiku Abubakar, has reacted to the abduction of the Principal of Government Secondary School, Odo-Ekina; a National Examinations Council, NECO, ad hoc official; and students who were writing their NECO examinati
